goal: is_cauchy_metric[Complex](q) = forall(x0: Real) { x0.is_positive implies exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[Complex](q, k0, x0) } }
proof: function[T0: lib(metric_space).MetricSpace](x0: Nat -> T0) { forall(x1: Real) { not x1.is_positive or exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[T0](x0, k0, x1) } } = is_cauchy_metric[T0](x0) }[Complex](q)

goal: eps.is_positive implies exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[Complex](q, k0, eps) }
proof: function(x0: Real) { not x0.is_positive or exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[Complex](q, k0, x0) } }(eps)

goal: cauchy_metric_bound[Complex](q, n, eps) and n <= i and n <= j

proof: function(x0: Nat -> Complex, x1: Nat) { re_seq(x0, x1) = x0(x1).re }(q, i)
function(x0: Nat -> Complex, x1: Nat) { re_seq(x0, x1) = x0(x1).re }(q, j)

goal: real_distance(re_seq(q, i), re_seq(q, j)) = (re_seq(q, i) - re_seq(q, j)).abs
proof: function(x0: Real, x1: Real) { (x0 - x1).abs = real_distance(x0, x1) }(re_seq(q, i), re_seq(q, j))

goal: re_seq(q, i).distance(re_seq(q, j)) = real_distance(re_seq(q, i), re_seq(q, j))

proof: let w0: Nat satisfy { exists(k0: Nat) { n <= w0 and n <= k0 and not re_seq(q, w0).distance(re_seq(q, k0)) < eps } }
let w1: Nat satisfy { n <= w0 and n <= w1 and not re_seq(q, w0).distance(re_seq(q, w1)) < eps }
let w2: Nat satisfy { n <= w0 and n <= w2 and not re_seq(q, w0).distance(re_seq(q, w2)) < eps }
function(x0: Nat, x1: Nat) { not n <= x0 or not n <= x1 or re_seq(q, x0).distance(re_seq(q, x1)) < eps }(w0, w2)
function(x0: Nat) { not cauchy_metric_bound(re_seq(q), x0, eps) }(n)

function[T0: lib(metric_space).MetricSpace](x0: Nat -> T0) { not forall(x1: Real) { not x1.is_positive or exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[T0](x0, k0, x1) } } or is_cauchy_metric[T0](x0) }[Real](re_seq(q))

goal: is_cauchy_metric[Complex](q) = forall(x0: Real) { x0.is_positive implies exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[Complex](q, k0, x0) } }
proof: function[T0: lib(metric_space).MetricSpace](x0: Nat -> T0) { forall(x1: Real) { not x1.is_positive or exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[T0](x0, k0, x1) } } = is_cauchy_metric[T0](x0) }[Complex](q)

goal: eps.is_positive implies exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[Complex](q, k0, eps) }
proof: function(x0: Real) { not x0.is_positive or exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[Complex](q, k0, x0) } }(eps)

goal: cauchy_metric_bound[Complex](q, n, eps) and n <= i and n <= j

proof: function(x0: Nat -> Complex, x1: Nat) { im_seq(x0, x1) = x0(x1).im }(q, i)
function(x0: Nat -> Complex, x1: Nat) { im_seq(x0, x1) = x0(x1).im }(q, j)

goal: real_distance(im_seq(q, i), im_seq(q, j)) = (im_seq(q, i) - im_seq(q, j)).abs
proof: function(x0: Real, x1: Real) { (x0 - x1).abs = real_distance(x0, x1) }(im_seq(q, i), im_seq(q, j))

goal: im_seq(q, i).distance(im_seq(q, j)) = real_distance(im_seq(q, i), im_seq(q, j))

proof: let w0: Nat satisfy { exists(k0: Nat) { n <= w0 and n <= k0 and not im_seq(q, w0).distance(im_seq(q, k0)) < eps } }
let w1: Nat satisfy { n <= w0 and n <= w1 and not im_seq(q, w0).distance(im_seq(q, w1)) < eps }
let w2: Nat satisfy { n <= w0 and n <= w2 and not im_seq(q, w0).distance(im_seq(q, w2)) < eps }
function(x0: Nat, x1: Nat) { not n <= x0 or not n <= x1 or im_seq(q, x0).distance(im_seq(q, x1)) < eps }(w0, w2)
function(x0: Nat) { not cauchy_metric_bound(im_seq(q), x0, eps) }(n)

function[T0: lib(metric_space).MetricSpace](x0: Nat -> T0) { not forall(x1: Real) { not x1.is_positive or exists(k0: Nat) { cauchy_metric_bound[T0](x0, k0, x1) } } or is_cauchy_metric[T0](x0) }[Real](im_seq(q))
function(x0: Real, x1: Real) { Complex.new(x0, x1).re = x0 }(a_re, a_im)
function(x0: Real, x1: Real) { Complex.new(x0, x1).im = x1 }(a_re, a_im)
function(x0: Complex) { not tendsto_metric(q, x0) }(a)

goal: forall(x0: Nat -> Complex) { is_cauchy_metric[Complex](x0) implies exists(k0: Complex) { tendsto_metric(x0, k0) } }
proof: let w0: Nat -> Complex satisfy { is_cauchy_metric[Complex](w0) and forall(x0: Complex) { not tendsto_metric(w0, x0) } }
function(x0: Nat -> Complex) { not is_cauchy_metric[Complex](x0) or exists(k0: Complex) { tendsto_metric(x0, k0) } }(w0)
function[T0](x0: T0 -> Bool) { not forall(x1: T0) { not x0(x1) } or not exists(k0: T0) { x0(k0) } }[Complex](tendsto_metric[Complex](w0))
